template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — GENERIC LETTER OF REPRESENTATION**:
A short, formal notice that this office now represents the client in the matter described. One page maximum, 2–3 body paragraphs.
DO NOT use section headers, Roman numerals, numbered sections, tables, or bullet points.
DO NOT generate a document title. DO NOT use markdown headings.

**WHAT THIS TEMPLATE IS**:
This is the BARE-BONES, practice-area-agnostic representation letter used when no more-specific template applies (e.g., an unusual practice area, a jurisdiction we don't yet have a pack for, or when the attorney wants a minimal letter without PI-specific language). Do not assume any particular practice area. Do not cite statutes unless one is explicitly supplied in the JURISDICTION CONTEXT and the attorney has asked for it.

**STRUCTURE**:

1. Date line — today in "Month Day, Year" format.

2. VIA line (bold) — `**VIA Email to [recipient email]**` if known, else `**VIA Certified Mail — Return Receipt Requested**`.

3. Recipient address block (separate lines):
   [Recipient name / organization]
   [Attention line if applicable]
   [Address]

4. Re: block (multi-line, aligned labels):
   Re:    Our Client(s):       [client name]
          Matter:              [brief matter description — pull from case data]
          [Optional]           [reference or claim number if present]

5. Greeting — "Dear [Title + last name]," if the recipient's name is known, otherwise "Dear Sir or Madam:" (for institutional recipients) or "Dear Counsel:" (for opposing counsel).

6. BODY PARAGRAPH 1 — Notice of Representation (1–2 sentences):
   State that this office represents the client in the described matter. Briefly identify the matter from the case data. Example: "Please be advised that this office represents [client name] in connection with [brief matter description]. All future communications regarding this matter should be directed to the undersigned."

7. BODY PARAGRAPH 2 — Optional follow-up request (1 paragraph, only if relevant):
   If the attorney has indicated a specific request (records, documents, status update, preservation of evidence), include one concise paragraph covering it. If no such request is specified, OMIT this paragraph entirely.

8. BODY PARAGRAPH 3 — Contact (ALWAYS the final body paragraph, 1–2 sentences):
   "Please contact the undersigned at [attorney phone] or [attorney email] with any questions or required follow-up. I look forward to your cooperation."
   If attorney contact info is not available, say "please contact this office."

9. Closing:
   - If stationery signature block is applied: STOP after the final body paragraph.
   - If no stationery: write "Very truly yours," then STOP.

**ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS**:
- Do NOT invent statutory citations. If the attorney wants statute-level detail, they should use a practice-area- or jurisdiction-specific LOR template instead.
- Do NOT assume a personal-injury context (no policy limits, no PIP, no UIM, no claim number unless it is explicitly present in the case data).
- Do NOT exceed one page.
- Total body text: 100–250 words.
hints: Bare, practice-area-agnostic representation letter. Used when no more-specific template matches. Two to three short paragraphs: representation notice, optional follow-up request, contact. No statutory citations. One page.
